name: Hyderabad Chicken Dum Biryani
description: A fragrant Hyderabadi-style chicken dum biryani made with marinated chicken, partially cooked basmati rice, fried onions, saffron milk, herbs, and slow steam cooking.
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup cooking oil or ghee, plus more as needed
2 tablespoons rose water or kewra water, optional
1 teaspoon shahi jeera or cumin seeds

recipeInstructions.name: Wash and soak the rice
recipeInstructions.text: Rinse the basmati rice until the water runs mostly clear. Soak it in water for 30 minutes, then drain.

recipeInstructions.name: Fry the onions
recipeInstructions.text: Heat oil or ghee in a pan and fry the sliced onions over medium heat until golden brown and crisp. Remove and drain on paper towels. Reserve some frying oil or ghee for layering.

recipeInstructions.name: Marinate the chicken
recipeInstructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken, yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, green chilies, lemon juice, turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala, biryani masala, salt, half of the fried onions, half of the mint, half of the cilantro, and 2 tablespoons of the reserved onion oil or ghee. Mix well and marinate for at least 1 hour, or overnight in the refrigerator for best flavor.

recipeInstructions.name: Prepare saffron milk
recipeInstructions.text: Soak saffron strands in warm milk for 10 to 15 minutes. Set aside.

recipeInstructions.name: Parboil the rice
recipeInstructions.text: Bring 8 cups water to a boil. Add cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, bay leaves, star anise if using, shahi jeera, and salt. Add the drained rice and cook until it is about 70 percent done, firm in the center but partly cooked. Drain immediately.

recipeInstructions.name: Layer the biryani
recipeInstructions.text: Place the marinated chicken in a heavy-bottomed pot. Spread it evenly. Add the parboiled rice on top. Sprinkle remaining fried onions, mint, cilantro, ghee, saffron milk, and rose water or kewra water if using.

recipeInstructions.name: Seal for dum cooking
recipeInstructions.text: Cover the pot tightly with a lid. Seal the edges with dough or place a heavy object on the lid to trap steam.

recipeInstructions.name: Cook on dum
recipeInstructions.text: Cook on medium-high heat for 8 to 10 minutes, then reduce heat to very low and cook for 35 to 45 minutes. Alternatively, place the pot on a hot griddle or tawa over low heat to prevent burning.

recipeInstructions.name: Rest and serve
recipeInstructions.text: Turn off the heat and let the biryani rest, covered, for 10 to 15 minutes. Gently fluff from the sides to mix rice and chicken without breaking the grains. Serve hot with raita, mirchi ka salan, or salad.
